L1.area: Memory buffer I/O abstractions and data retrieval utilities

L2.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists where a raw pointer, which may be null when a memory buffer is empty, is unconditionally passed to a slice construction function that requires a non-null pointer argument. This leads to undefined behavior, and in debug builds triggers an assertion failure.

L3.area: Memory BIO (Basic I/O) buffer abstraction and its data retrieval logic
L3.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists where a raw pointer obtained from an OpenSSL memory BIO data retrieval call, which is null when the buffer is empty, is unconditionally passed to a Rust slice construction function that requires a non-null pointer argument. This leads to undefined behavior, and in debug builds triggers an assertion failure.

ground_truth.reason: MemBio::get_buf calls slice::from_raw_parts with a potentially null pointer when the BIO buffer is empty, violating the function's non-null invariant and causing undefined behavior.
